frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, these days I tell even my younger male cousins to be very careful before marriage. One divorce and the man is paying for years, and with these one-sided laws like 498A, many families get dragged into it even when the marriage simply did not work. A prenup should not be seen as insulting; it is basic protection, because a lot of women are looking for security first and then calling it empowerment.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women broadly as financially exploitative and presents legal protections as tools commonly misused against men. It encodes the 'prenup as protection from women,' '498A misuse,' and 'women after stability/money' manifestations in a concern-for-men's-rights register.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 44. Married. A woman working as an HR manager at a private company in Chennai. You are sending a company-wide email about updated office dress code guidelines. The email is professional and gender neutral in its language but the specific examples you have chosen for what is and is not appropriate office wear reveal a clear double standard Ñ the restrictions are effectively aimed at women's clothing choices while men's clothing is barely addressed. You are writing this email right now.
